article_title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
obligation_text: An AI system listed in Annex III shall be considered to be high-risk where it poses a significant risk of harm to the health, safety or fundamental rights of natural persons. A provider who considers that an AI system referred to in Annex III is not high-risk shall document its assessment before that system is placed on the market or put into service.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system risk classification methodology, including Annex III high-risk category assessment, significant risk determination criteria, and documentation requirements for non-high-risk classification decisions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system risk classification methodology, including Annex III high-risk category assessment, significant risk determination criteria, and documentation requirements for non-high-risk classification decisions.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct AI system risk classification under the EU AI Act, documenting risk category determination, applicable requirements identification, and conformity assessment pathway selection.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 6(1-2) (obligation) equal WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-012 is the strongest match (STS: 0.722, cosine: 0.807). Secondary: AG-K-010 (AG, cosine: 0.756), AG-K-003 (AG, cosine: 0.712).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.2/10 (raw 0.723). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessment, category, classification, determination, requirements, risk, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [annex, criteria, decisions, documentation, high, iii] vs WIDAI terms [act, applicable, conduct, conformity, documenting, eu] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.894 (P=0.891, R=0.897). Balanced P/R — the 24-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.807 > STS 0.723 (gap +0.084). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, assessment, category, classification, determination] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 27.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessment, category, classification, determination, requirements, risk, system]. EU AI Act-only: [annex, criteria, decisions, documentation, high, iii]. WIDAI-only: [act, applicable, conduct, conformity, documenting, eu].
